prompt: A **Neighborhood Commerce & Content Operations Management System** that includes community-based fulfillment administration, product discovery, inventory control, structured content management, real-time messaging, and operational governance for a local group-leader commerce model. The backend exposes resource-oriented API capabilities for Authentication and Roles (local username/password only, password minimum 12 characters with salted hashing, lockout for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts), Communities and Service Areas (create/update community locations using US address fields, bind one active group leader per community, configure service hours and a fulfillment scope), Group-Leader Contracts (commission rules per product category with defaults such as 6.0% commission, configurable floor/ceiling 0–15%, and a settlement cycle of weekly or biweekly with a 2-day dispute window), Catalog and Search (keyword search with autocomplete, persisted local search history per user capped at 50 entries, “trending” computed from the last 7 days of local queries, filters for price range in USD, brand, and attribute tags, sorting by sales volume/price/new arrivals, pagination with a maximum page size of 100, and a zero-result guidance response that returns closest-match brands/tags). Inventory and Warehouse APIs support multi-warehouse and bin locations, barcode/RFID identifiers as strings (format-validated only), lot/serial tracking, receipts/issues/transfers, cycle counts with variance reasons, safety-stock alerts when on-hand drops below a per-SKU threshold, slow-moving flags when no issue occurs for 60 days, and costing using FIFO or moving-average per SKU (immutable once transactions exist). Messaging APIs provide WebSocket/STOMP-based direct and group messaging with message types for text, image/file metadata (no external storage), emoji, and system notices, plus offline queues with redelivery for up to 7 days and delivery/read receipts. Content and Template APIs manage articles/books/chapters with Markdown and rich-text stored as sanitized text, draft-to-publish workflow, version control, tags/categories, cover/attachment assets stored locally, and capture templates with field dictionaries/enums, publish/rollback, and compatibility rules so older versions remain parseable; template migrations must be additive or provide deterministic mapping rules.The service runs as a single-machine offline deployment (Docker-friendly) using **Flask** to implement the API layer, **SQLAlchemy** for data access, and **SQLite** for persistence with explicit transactional boundaries for inventory movements, settlements, and content publishing. Core tables include Users (unique username, role enum: Administrator, Operations Manager, Moderator, Group Leader, Staff, Member), Communities, ServiceAreas, GroupLeaderBindings (unique community_id where active=true), CommissionRules (scoped by community/product category), SettlementRuns (idempotency key required per run), Products, ProductAttributes/Tags, SearchLogs, Warehouses/Bins, InventoryLots, InventoryTransactions (indexed by sku_id, warehouse_id, occurred_at), CostLayers (for FIFO) or AvgCostSnapshots (for moving average), ContentItems and ContentVersions, Attachments (sha256 fingerprint, size limit 25 MB, allowed types: png/jpg/pdf/txt/md), CaptureTemplates and TemplateVersions, Messages and MessageReceipts, and AdminTickets/Reports. Access control is enforced server-side with role checks and row-level scoping (e.g., group leaders can only query performance and order attribution for their bound communities), audit trails are required for moderation actions, settlement changes, and inventory adjustments (immutable append-only log). Sensitive fields (password hashes, any payout identifiers) must be encrypted at rest using a locally stored key and masked in logs; logs must exclude message bodies by default. Non-functional requirements include 99th percentile API latency under 300 ms for search queries on a dataset of 50,000 products on a single workstation, background job reliability with retry/backoff for message redelivery, and observability with structured logs, correlation IDs, and traceable spans across messaging, search, and inventory flows; canary and rollback are supported via database migration versioning and application build version tags, ensuring safe rollback without breaking historical template parsing or inventory costing integrity.
